assertions.title: Structural Parity â€” All Python Functions Have Go Counterparts

steps.expected: 9 exported Go functions covering the yj_book.py pipeline
steps.observed: Found 9 functions: mergeContentFragmentStringSymbols, resolveSharedSymbol, mergeIonReferencedStringSymbols, sharedDocSymbols.update, sharedDocSymbols.get, bookState.fragmentSnapshot, buildBookState, buildBookStateFromData, organizeFragments

steps.expected: All needed methods have Go counterparts; excluded methods are convert_to_single_kfx, convert_to_cbz, convert_to_pdf, convert_to_kpf, convert_to_zip_unpack, convert_to_json_content
steps.observed: All 12 needed methods mapped: __init__â†’types, load_symbol_catalogâ†’sharedTable(), decode_bookâ†’buildBookState+organizeFragments, locate_book_datafilesâ†’loadBookSources, locate_files_from_dirâ†’filepath.WalkDir, check_located_fileâ†’inline, get_containerâ†’loadContainerSourceData, expand_compressed_containerâ†’decryptDRMION, final_actionsâ†’N/A, convert_to_epubâ†’ConvertFile, get_metadataâ†’ExtractSidecarMetadata. Also: organize_fragments_by_typeâ†’organizeFragments, replace_ion_dataâ†’mergeIonReferencedStringSymbols+mergeContentFragmentStringSymbols. 6 Calibre output modes excluded by design.

assertions.details: All 12 needed Python methods have Go counterparts. 6 Calibre-only output mode methods (convert_to_single_kfx, convert_to_cbz, convert_to_pdf, convert_to_kpf, convert_to_zip_unpack, convert_to_json_content) correctly excluded by design. Function mapping documented in yj_book.go header (commit 4aa8c86).

assertions.title: Behavioral Parity â€” Fragment Organization
assertions.details: organizeFragments correctly categorizes content fragments, remaps container format ($270), reuses last container ID ($593), handles font count suffix ($262), and handles $387 with $215 suffix. buildBookState produces fragment summaries matching reference for all test fixtures.

assertions.title: No Regression â€” Go Tests Pass

steps.observed: Exits 0 with no output â€” clean

steps.expected: All tests pass (with known pre-existing TestConvertFileMatchesReferenceStructureIgnoringImages exception)
steps.observed: All tests PASS except TestConvertFileMatchesReferenceStructureIgnoringImages (FAIL) â€” this is the known pre-existing image tiling failure unrelated to M1 yj_book changes. Total 9.083s.

assertions.details: go vet: clean (exit 0, no warnings). Full test suite: only TestConvertFileMatchesReferenceStructureIgnoringImages fails â€” this is the known pre-existing image tiling issue documented in the validation contract. M1-specific tests (TestBuildBookState*, TestOrganizeFragments*) all pass. The M1 changes (commits 338b3ef, 4aa8c86) are refactoring + documentation only â€” no functional code changes that could cause regressions.

assertions.title: No Regression â€” Original 6 Books Unchanged

steps.observed: parity_check.sh reports FAIL with differences in organize_fragments stage (Python uses $N symbols, Go uses real names â€” expected format difference) and content rendering stages. However, these differences are pre-existing and NOT caused by M1 changes.

steps.expected: No functional code changes in M1 commits that affect EPUB output
steps.observed: git diff c66a658..HEAD -- internal/kfx/ shows only doc.go and yj_book.go comment changes. No functional Go code was modified. EPUB output is identical pre/post M1.

assertions.details: The parity_check.sh script shows differences, but these are pre-existing baseline differences (heading IDs, CSS class naming, fragment type naming format) that existed before M1 changes were added. The M1 changes (commits 338b3ef, 4aa8c86) only add documentation comments to yj_book.go and doc.go â€” zero functional code changes. EPUB output for Martyr is byte-identical before and after M1. Manual EPUB comparison (Go output vs Calibre reference) shows 87/114 files match, 27 differ â€” these are the known heading ID and content rendering gaps unrelated to yj_book.

frictions.description: scripts/diff_kfx_parity.sh uses 'python' command which is not available on this system (only python3)
frictions.resolution: Used manual EPUB comparison with python3 instead

frictions.description: scripts/parity_check.sh organize_fragments stage reports mismatch because Python traces use $N symbol names while Go traces use real human-readable names â€” this is an expected format difference, not a functional parity gap
frictions.resolution: Recognized as expected behavior based on AGENTS.md documentation about Go using real names vs Python using $N placeholders

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all 4 passed. VAL-M1-001: all needed Python functions have Go counterparts (12 mapped, 6 excluded by design). VAL-M1-002: 6 fragment organization tests pass. VAL-M1-003: go vet clean, only pre-existing image tiling test fails. VAL-M1-004: M1 changes are documentation-only, EPUB output unchanged from baseline.
